message:
style(fcp-core): apply rustfmt across fourteen pinned-test files

Why
---
Fourteen test files in crates/fcp-core/tests/ had drifted from the
project's rustfmt configuration. This is the third sweep of the
same kind, following commits e85d8b7d9 and 9de8b549c. The pattern
is unchanged: bead-driven test-pin work focuses on what to assert
rather than how the file should be formatted, so files
periodically accumulate import-ordering, line-width, and method-chain
patterns that rustfmt re-flows. Sweeping them keeps `cargo fmt
--check` quiet on CI and prevents whitespace-only diffs from
showing up alongside future test-pin work in these same files.

Each diff in this commit is mechanically reproducible: re-running
cargo fmt against any of these files produces no further output.
Zero assertions added or removed, zero fixture values changed,
zero new tests, zero new imports beyond what rustfmt re-orders.

What
----
Files touched (all pure rustfmt output):

- capability_token_display_roundtrip.rs
- connector_selector_ordering.rs
- intent_manifest_serde_tag_matrix.rs
- lease_renewal_reason_display.rs
- mesh_discovery_serde_matrix.rs
- operation_descriptor_serde_tag_matrix.rs
- policy_conflict_resolution_variant_display.rs
- policy_evaluation_context_serde.rs
- principal_kind_serde.rs
- registry_sync_state_machine.rs
- scope_attributes_serde.rs
- selector_mode_variant_matrix.rs
- zone_route_hint_variant_display.rs
- zone_transition_state_machine.rs

Typical patterns rustfmt re-flowed:

- Import groups reordered to put types before submodule items
  (e.g. `use serde::{de::DeserializeOwned, Serialize}` ->
  `use serde::{Serialize, de::DeserializeOwned}`).
- `use serde_json::{json, Value}` -> `use serde_json::{Value, json}`
  to match the project's import-order convention.
- Single-line builder calls broken across multiple lines once
  argument count pushes line-width past the budget
  (e.g. `principal("agent", "planner", TrustLevel::Admin,
  Some("Planner"))` wrapping each arg onto its own line).
- Method-chain calls split at the dot when inline form exceeds
  width (e.g. `counts.entry(...).or_insert(0)` becoming three
  lines).
- Tuple/array literal entries that exceed the line-width budget
  reformatted one element per line.

There is no path through these files where behaviour can change
as a result of rustfmt reflowing whitespace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
